title: rhaphiolepis williamtelliana
intro: Rhaphiolepis williamtelliana, also known as the pink azalea or woolly rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a shrub native to China, valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Rhaphiolepis williamtelliana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rhaphiolepis williamtelliana can be made from its roots, leaves, and flowers, often used in traditional Chinese medicine to create teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal purposes.
